channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: IAEA reports over 160 drones near Ukrainian nuclear sites amid safety concerns
item.description: The International Atomic Energy Agency documented a surge in drone activity near Khmelnytskyi, Rivne, and South Ukraine nuclear power plants, raising nuclear safety risks as Zaporizhzhia plant operates on a single backup power line for over seven weeks.

item.title: China renews US beef import licenses, raising competition for Brazil
item.description: Beijing restored import licenses for hundreds of US beef plants during the Trump-Xi summit, reversing months of frozen trade and creating new competition for Brazilian exporters in Chinas largest beef market.

item.title: Philippine senator wanted by ICC escapes Senate custody amid gunfire
item.description: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court for alleged crimes against humanity, fled the heavily guarded Senate building after gunshots were fired during an argument between security and a government agent.

item.title: UK Health Minister Streeting resigns, calls for Labour leadership contest
item.description: Wes Streeting became the first senior minister to quit, calling for a leadership contest to replace Prime Minister Keir Starmer following disastrous local election results that have plunged British politics into crisis.

item.title: Lebanon demands Israel cease-fire at Washington talks
item.description: Lebanese officials are pressing for an immediate cease-fire in face-to-face talks with Israel in Washington, as Hezbollah and Israel continue trading blows despite a US-backed truce declared last month.

item.title: Iran war overshadows BRICS foreign ministers meeting
item.description: Tensions from the Iran conflict dominated discussions at a BRICS meeting, highlighting how the crisis is straining relations even within the bloc of major emerging economies.

item.title: China expresses interest in buying more US oil after Trump-Xi talks
item.description: White House officials raised the prospect of increased Chinese purchases of American energy following the summit between Presidents Trump and Xi in Beijing.

item.title: Nvidia CEO hopes Trump-Xi meeting will improve US-China ties
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang expressed optimism that the Beijing summit would help repair frayed relations between Washington and Beijing amid ongoing technology tensions.

item.title: Global markets rally on Trump-Xi trade truce hopes
item.description: Stock markets worldwide climbed on expectations that the summit between the US and Chinese leaders would yield progress on reducing trade tensions.

item.title: UK deputy PM Rayner cleared in tax investigation
item.description: British authorities cleared Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner over tax affairs that had become a political liability for the Labour government.

item.title: Cuba runs out of diesel fuel amid US blockade
item.description: Cuba's energy minister warned the island nation has exhausted its diesel fuel reserves, blaming a US blockade for preventing fuel shipments from reaching the country.

item.title: Pound dips as political crisis engulfs Britain
item.description: The British pound fell against major currencies as internal Labour Party turmoil threatens to destabilize the government.

item.title: IMF says constructive US-China dialogue reduced tensions
item.description: The International Monetary Fund welcomed signs of improved dialogue between Washington and Beijing, saying reduced tensions benefit the global economy.

item.title: Activist flotilla leaves Turkey for Gaza
item.description: A new activist flotilla departed from Turkey toward Gaza, weeks after Israel intercepted a previous convoy attempting to break the naval blockade.

item.title: Trump-Xi summit exposes gaps on Iran, Taiwan
item.description: The Beijing summit between Trump and Xi revealed diverging priorities, with China remaining silent on Iran while the US avoided mentioning Taiwan in official statements.

item.title: Zelensky orders military response after Russian attack kills 12 in Kyiv
item.description: Ukraine's president vowed retaliation after a deadly Russian strike on Kyiv killed at least 12 people and wounded dozens more in the latest escalation of the war.

item.title: Germany's Merz: Russia prefers escalation over negotiation
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized Russia's stance, saying Moscow continues to choose military escalation rather than pursuing diplomatic solutions.

item.title: Cisco and LinkedIn announce major layoffs
item.description: Technology companies Cisco and LinkedIn announced significant workforce reductions as the industry continues to adjust to changing market conditions.

item.title: Russia's energy exports cushion blow from Iran war fallout
item.description: Increased Russian energy exports are helping Moscow weather the economic impact of sanctions and the broader Iran conflict disruption to global markets.

item.title: Pope Leo decries European military spending as betrayal of diplomacy
item.description: The new pope criticized Europe's military spending priorities, arguing that increased defense budgets represent a failure of diplomatic efforts.

item.title: Trump wants China's help on Iran, but Beijing may have other ideas
item.description: US officials are seeking Chinese influence to help resolve the Iran crisis, though Beijing's priorities may not align with Washington's objectives.

item.title: Trump and Xi meet in Beijing with trade truce and Iran war at stake
item.description: The US and Chinese leaders held high-stakes talks in Beijing focused on trade relations and the ongoing Iran conflict, with global markets watching closely.

item.title: Iran and UAE clash at BRICS foreign ministers meeting
item.description: Tensions flared between Iran and the United Arab Emirates during the BRICS foreign ministers gathering, highlighting regional strains within the bloc.

item.title: Iran war overshadows Trump's China visit as peace talks stall
item.description: The ongoing Iran conflict dominated the agenda during Trump's visit to Beijing, with diplomatic efforts to resolve the crisis showing little progress.

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine for third consecutive day
item.description: Russian forces continued their assault on Ukraine, striking Kyiv and other targets in the third day of intensified attacks.
